messages.content: переделай это предложение в страдательный залог: Design is the creative activity of creating an aesthetic appearance for various things.
An aesthetic appearance for various things is created through the creative activity of design. (by someone/something)
переделай это предложение в страдательный залог:  The basic elements of good design are minimalism and practicality
Minimalism and practicality are considered the basic elements of good design. (by someone/something)

сделай из этого условное предложение: Under his leadership, Apple has become one of the most valuable companies in the world, and Ive himself is responsible for the design of many of the company's iconic products
If he had not led Apple, Apple would not have become one of the most valuable companies in the world, and Ive would not have been responsible for designing many of the company's iconic products.

сделай из этого согласование времен в косвенной речи: This led to the creation of the iPhone, which revolutionized the way we interact with technology and made it available to people all over the world.
He said that this had led to the creation of the iPhone, which had revolutionized the way people interacted with technology and had made it available to people all over the world.
сделай из этого согласование времен в косвенной речи:  We meet design in the interiors of houses, on the streets, in flash mobs and buildings.
He said that design was encountered in the interiors of houses, on the streets, in flash mobs, and buildings.

сделай из этого согласование времен в косвенной речи: He believed that the best designs should be simple and intuitive and should allow users to focus on what they are trying to achieve rather than the technology itself.
He believed that the best designs should be simple and intuitive and should allow users to focus on what they were trying to achieve rather than the technology itself.
сделай из этого модальные глаголы для выражения умозаключения (present and past deduction): After all that has been said, we understand that Design surrounds us everywhere.

Present Deduction: 

- Design must surround us everywhere after all that has been said. 
- Design should be surrounding us everywhere after all that has been said. 
- Design may be surrounding us everywhere after all that has been said. 

Past Deduction: 

- Design must have surrounded us everywhere after all that had been said. 
- Design should have been surrounding us everywhere after all that had been said. 
- Design may have been surrounding us everywhere after all that had been said.
сделай из этого модальные глаголы для выражения умозаключения (present and past deduction): Summing up, I would like to say that design has been an integral part of society since ancient times.

Present Deduction: 

- Design must be an integral part of society since ancient times. 
- Design should be an integral part of society since ancient times. 
- Design may be an integral part of society since ancient times. 

Past Deduction: 

- Design must have been an integral part of society since ancient times. 
- Design should have been an integral part of society since ancient times. 
- Design may have been an integral part of society since ancient times.

сделай из этого модальные глаголы для выражения умозаключения (present and past deduction): Good design always appeal to somebody because it is excellent value for money, meets the needs of the user, and well designed things are made of durable material such as metal.

Present Deduction: 

- Good design must always appeal to somebody because it is excellent value for money, meets the needs of the user, and well-designed things are made of durable materials such as metal. 
- Good design should always appeal to somebody because it is excellent value for money, meets the needs of the user, and well-designed things are made of durable materials such as metal. 
- Good design may always appeal to somebody because it is excellent value for money, meets the needs of the user, and well-designed things are made of durable materials such as metal. 

Past Deduction: 

- Good design must have always appealed to somebody because it was excellent value for money, met the needs of the user, and well-designed things were made of durable materials such as metal. 
- Good design should have always appealed to somebody because it was excellent value for money, met the needs of the user, and well-designed things were made of durable materials such as metal. 
- Good design may have always appealed to somebody because it was excellent value for money, met the needs of the user, and well-designed things were made of durable materials such as metal.

напиши текст по плану: Step 1. Introduction
1. Start with a hook sentence that will attract the listener’s attention (a quote, a proverb, etc.).
2. Lead your speech steadily to the main part of your talk.
3. The introduction may consist of 3-6 sentences.
Step 2. What is advertising? What Makes a Good Advert?
2.1. What is advertising? Do advertisements/commercials influence your choice of goods and services?

2.2. What makes a good advert? Describe some good/bad advert explaining its success/failure.

Step 1. Introduction

"Advertising is the art of convincing people to spend money they don't have for something they don't really need." This statement, made by American humorist Will Rogers, may seem cynical, but it's not far off the truth. Advertising is everywhere around us, bombarding us with messages about what we should buy, how we should look and how we should think. Today, I want to explore the world of advertising and what makes a good advert.

Step 2. What is advertising? What Makes a Good Advert?

2.1. Advertising is a form of communication that aims to persuade an audience to purchase or take action. In many ways, advertisements shape our choices and influence the way we see the world. Think about it: how often do you base your purchasing decisions on something you saw on TV or online?

2.2. So what makes a good advert? A good advert is one that connects with its audience, communicates a clear message, entertains or informs them, and ultimately produces a desired response. Think about the famous Coca-Cola commercials, with their catchy jingle and nostalgic feel. These ads connect with viewers on an emotional level and create a sense of loyalty and nostalgia.

On the other hand, bad adverts are the ones that fail to connect with their audience or are offensive or annoying. One example of a bad advert is the Pepsi commercial with Kendall Jenner, which was criticized for trivializing social justice movements.

In conclusion, advertising is a powerful tool that shapes our buying decisions and influences our perspectives. A good advert is one that connects with audiences, communicates a clear message and ultimately produces a desired response. As consumers, we must be mindful of the messages we consume and the impact they have on our lives.

напиши текст по плану: Step 1. Introduction
1. Start with a hook sentence that will attract the listener’s attention (a quote, a proverb, etc.).
2. Lead your speech steadily to the main part of your talk.
3. The introduction may consist of 3-6 sentences.		
Step 2. What is advertising? What Makes a Good Advert?
2.1. What is advertising? Do advertisements/commercials influence your choice of goods and services?

2.2. What makes a good advert? Describe some good/bad advert explaining its success/failure.
		
Step 3. Manipulating Images
3.1 Speak about the reasons for manipulating images in advertising.
3.2. What are the arguments “for” and “against”? What is your attitude to manipulating images of people in advertising?
		
Step 4. Advertising & Children/Young People
4.1. Speak about the ways which advertisers use to reach children and young people. Are there any dangers of advertising to children?
4.2. Speak about the measures that should be taken to control advertising  aimed at children and young people.

		
Step 5. CREATIVE THINKING
Introduce your own extra idea(s) on the topic that hasn’t/haven’t been mentioned before. Justify your choice.
		
Step 6. Conclusion
Summarise the ideas of steps 2,3,4,5.

Step 1. Introduction

“Advertising is legalized lying.” This quote by legendary copywriter H.G. Wells sums up the often-controversial world of advertising. Advertising has the power to influence our decisions, shape our beliefs and even change our culture. Today, we will explore the world of advertising and its impact on society.

Step 2. What is advertising? What Makes a Good Advert?

2.1. Advertising is a form of communication used to promote or sell products, services or ideas to an audience. But do advertisements and commercials really influence our choices when it comes to goods and services? We'll explore this question in more detail.

2.2. So what makes a good advertisement? A good ad is one that captures the attention of an audience, connects emotionally, communicates a clear message and motivates them to take action. Think about the iconic Apple ads, with their simple and clean design and sleek presentation. These ads communicate the product's features and benefits in an uncluttered and compelling way.

On the other hand, there are bad ads that miss the mark, are annoying or simply ineffective. Consider the old-fashioned infomercials that promise results that are too good to be true.

Step 3. Manipulating Images

3.1. Manipulating images is a common advertising practice, but what are the reasons behind it? Why do advertisers manipulate images and what are the effects?

3.2. The arguments for and against image manipulation in advertising are many, with some people arguing it's a way to make products more visually appealing and others calling out the dangers of promoting unrealistic body standards. What are your thoughts on image manipulation in advertising?

Step 4. Advertising & Children/Young People

4.1. Advertisers know that targeting children and young people is a lucrative market, often using catchy jingles and pop culture references to capture their attention. But what are the dangers of advertising to children, and how do advertisers use this knowledge to their advantage?

4.2. To protect children from advertising, some measures should be taken. What can be done to ensure that advertising aimed at children is fair, honest and non-exploitative?

Step 5. CREATIVE THINKING

As we have discussed, advertising is a controversial and complex topic. One extra idea may be to discuss how advertising is shaping our culture and beliefs, and whether it's a force for good or bad. What are the long-term effects of advertising on society and how should we interpret the messages conveyed to us?

Step 6. Conclusion

In conclusion, advertising is a ubiquitous aspect of modern society that shapes our attitudes and beliefs. A good advert is one that connects with audiences, communicates a clear message and motivates action. However, advertisers must be aware of the potential negative effects of advertising, particularly on children and young people. We must continue to question and evaluate the messages conveyed by advertising, to ensure it serves as a beneficial force in our society.
